sections.paragraph:
ἤπιος, α, ον (since Hom., also Empedokles; Vorsokr. 17, 18 [the rdg. in Ath. 22, 1], Epict.p. 487, 3; grave ins APF 5, 1913, 166 no. 17, 4 the deceased is decribed as ἤπιον ἀνθρώποισι; POxy 1380, 11; 86; 155; Philo, Mos. 1, 72; cp. DELG s.v.) gentle 1 Th 2:7 v.l. (for νήπιοι q.v.). ἤπ. πρός τινα kind toward someone 2 Ti 2:24 (v.l. νήπιον).—DELG. M-M. Spicq. Sv.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

Vorsokr. Vorsokr. = Vorsokratiker, their fragments, ed. by HDiels/WKranz (the term Pre-Socratic[s] is used when no specific ref. is made to the German ed.)—Lists 5, 6

rdg. rdg. = reading(s)

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

APF APF = Archiv für Papyrusforschung—Lists 4, 6

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

q.v. q.v. = quod vide (which see, see that which has just been mentioned)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 439
